_comment: CROSS-REPO PARITY GOLDEN for the plan_graph engine (A2.1 Piece 0a). The gtd plugin's scripts/plan_graph.py and the rtm-mcp server's src/rtm_mcp/plan_graph.py are byte-compatible-by-hand ports of one algorithm; this golden is the automated guard. BOTH repos carry an identical copy of this file and a test (gtd: test_plan_graph_parity.py; rtm-mcp: tests/test_plan_graph_parity.py) asserting build_graph(input.header, input.rows)'s thin surface (edges / judgement / order / cycles / fingerprint) == expected. If either engine drifts, its test fails. Regenerate ONLY when BOTH engines are changed in lockstep, and update BOTH copies. Generated 2026-06-27; regenerated 2026-07-05 for the MoSCoW band tie-break (within each tier: band Must->Should->Could->untriaged-last, then due/start, then input order; priority joins the fingerprint) — gtd v0.130.0 / rtm-mcp v1.22.0 lockstep. Rows now carry priority; row 4 (Must, undated) deliberately sorts above row 3 (Could, due-dated) within the ready tier, exercising band-beats-date. Fingerprint fe2a0a6e201881f6.
